name: Meänmaa (Tornedalians)

colors.symbolism: from the Swedish flag — the western side of the Torne valley
the Torne River; from the Finnish flag

description: Three equal horizontal bands of yellow (top), white (middle), and blue (bottom).
history: The flag of Meänmaa — the cultural region along the Torne River valley straddling the Swedish–Finnish border, home to the Tornedalian (Meänkieli-speaking) people — was designed by Bengt Pohjanen and adopted on 15 July 2007 at the founding of the Meänmaa cultural association in Pajala. Meänmaa Day (Meänflaakun päivä) is celebrated annually on 15 July. The flag is unofficial but widely flown by Tornedalian cultural organisations on both sides of the border.

features.symbolism: The yellow and blue evoke the colours of the Swedish flag and the white-and-blue of the Finnish flag — together representing the Tornedalian community that straddles the Sweden–Finland border along the Torne River valley. White also represents the snow.
